methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Extropic is a Series A chip and semiconductor company founded by Guillaume Verdon that has raised $50M+. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 4 same-stage, same-sector private peers (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) valued between $500M and $5.0B, alongside reference to 3 public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trading at a median 20.6x price-to-sales.

Two quantitative methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $50M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a standard 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $100M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 identified private comps yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with the sample ranging from $500M (Rain AI) to $5.0B (Etched). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures, which remain unavailable for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $85M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median implied value of $800M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Extropic's competitive position relative to peers and the nascent, highly volatile nature of the frontier-chip market segment. Key sensitivities include near-term technical milestones, customer traction, and the trajectory of peer valuations in this rapidly repricing sector.
